summary: Stellar Dark Star, Bird Song & jazzy second set with Branford Marsalis
blurb: The show delivers a monster Dark Star and soaring Bird Song, while the second set erupts into an extended jam suite featuring Eyes of the World, Estimated Prophet and a jazzy guest appearance by Branford Marsalis. Jerry’s guitar is on fire and Brent provides vibrant keyboards throughout. Highlights include the powerful Drum/Space transition and a heartfelt Knockin’ on Heaven’s Door encore.
review: On March 29, 1990 at Nassau Coliseum the Dead reached one of their late‑period peaks. The first set opens with a ripping "Jack Straw" that segues into a tight "Bertha", but it’s the mid‑set "Bird Song" that truly lifts the night—Garcia’s soaring lead and Brent’s bright piano make it unforgettable. The second set is where the magic explodes: an expansive "Eyes Of The World" flows straight into an inspired "Estimated Prophet" before launching into a monster dual‑Dark Star/Space sequence, amplified by Branford Marsalis’ jazzy saxophone. The rhythm section—Mickey and Bill—drive the improvisations with relentless intensity, while Phil’s bass is front‑and‑center, bubbling through every groove. Highlights ripple onward through "The Wheel", a fiery "Throwing Stones" > "Turn On Your Lovelight*" medley, and a heartfelt encore of "Knockin' On Heaven's Door" that caps an evening many fans still cite as legendary.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Across all recordings the sentiment is overwhelmingly positive: multiple reviewers call the show "legendary", praise the Dark Star, Bird Song and Eyes Of The World as "jaw‑dropping" or "must‑listen", and highlight Branford Marsalis' guest spot. Only a handful of minor criticisms (subdued vocals on one tape, a lyric slip on "We Can Run") appear, insufficient to offset the strong acclaim.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard source—crisp, clear, no drop‑outs, captures the full band and Branford Marsalis with superior instrument separation.

key_highlights: Monster dual‑Dark Star/Space sequence with jazzy saxophone
Soaring Bird Song and extended Eyes Of The World jam
Brilliant Estimated Prophet leading into a high‑energy second set

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia is on fire—soaring guitar lines, emotional vocals and a masterful command of the jam, especially on Dark Star and Bird Song.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provides a solid, prominent bass foundation that bubbles through the mix; his playing is described as "everywhere in the mix" and especially jazzy during the second set.
band_performance.Bob: Weir delivers strong rhythm guitar work and contributes powerful backup vocals, notably on Estimated Prophet and the jam sections.
band_performance.Brent: Mydland’s keyboards are vibrant and jazzy, fitting perfectly with Branford's guest spot; his playing is described as "ON", "ballistic" and a key driver of the second‑set flow.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart’s drumming adds depth and intensity, particularly during the powerful Drum/Space transition after Dark Star.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann locks in with tight, driving beats that propel the extended jams; his work is praised as part of the "solid rhythm section" throughout.
